items.title: The AMPA receptor life cycle: assembly, regulation and synaptic diversity
items.author: Amanda M. Perozzo
items.content: AMPA receptors (AMPARs) mediate fast excitatory neurotransmission in the brain. Derek Bowie and colleagues describe recent advances in our understanding of the regulatory layers that operate across the lifespan of an AMPAR to govern its assembly, structure and function.

items.title: Resolving rapid cell-surface proteome remodelling in intact neural circuits
items.author: Colleen N. McLaughlin
items.content: In this Tools of the Trade article, Colleen McLaughlin describes endocytome profiling, a systematic and quantitative approach for monitoring of cell-surface protein remodelling in the intact brain.

items.title: When inhibition organizes the brain
items.author: Renata Batista-Brito
items.content: In this Journal Club, Renata Batista-Brito discusses a 2009 study that showed that a population of inhibitory hub neurons orchestrate network synchrony in the developing hippocampus.

items.title: Metabolic vulnerability and tau pathology in combination drive necroptosis
items.author: Sian Lewis
items.content: Neuronal loss in a mouse model of Alzheimer disease occurs through necroptosis and is shown to be dependent on low-glucose conditions, which increase hyperphosphorylated tau levels; in turn, this simultaneously activates pro-necroptotic pathways and disables a key checkpoint mechanism.
